title: Ring Bandage
content_markdown:
#### Indications

- To control bleeding caused by penetrating injury and secure the penetrating object in place.

#### Procedure

Figure 1

Figure 2

Figure 3

- Using a triangular bandage, roll up so that each “tying” end is at greatest distance from each other.

- Form a loose overhand knot at the centre so that a small loop is formed (see **Figure 1**).

- Wrap “tying” ends in and around the knot to form a doughnut (see **Figures 2 & 3**).

- Position ring bandage around penetrating object.

- Secure in place with appropriate bandage.

- Ensure there is no pressure being applied to the penetrating object and that the ring bandage is not touching the penetrating object.
